title: CCTP/SDDB: Systemic Tau and ordinal RECD before spontaneous ventricular fibrillation
description:
Analysis code, cleaned RR series, results, and figures for the manuscript Context-Dependent Relational Reorganization of Heart Rate Dynamics Precedes Spontaneous Ventricular Fibrillation: Systemic Tau and Ordinal RECD Evidence from the Sudden Cardiac Death Holter Database.

v1.3.0 adds exploratory native ordinal alarm detectors as methodological alternatives to the frozen continuous abs-z rule, integrated as manuscript §3.11: Ordinal Persistence Collapse (OPC) and Symbolic Distribution Divergence (SDD) on joint Bandt–Pompe symbols (K=36); sensitivity–FAR trade-off on SDDB+VFDB (n=33 events) vs NSRDB (n=18 controls); light causal cascade SDD→OPC (±5 min); modest OPC parameter grid with keep baseline (L=50, θD=0.35, θR=5). Presented as a trade-off surface, not superiority. No clinical / S5 / FDA / deployability claim. Production abs-z remains frozen (θ3=0.08, high-threshold=0.65, Wτ=101, abs-z≥2×3).

Prior releases: v1.2.0 Phase 2 public NSRDB FAR interim; v1.1.0 Phase 1 external validation; SDDB discovery N=10 foundational.

Pipeline: RR extraction from PhysioNet SDDB, Systemic Tau (τs), phase-shuffle surrogates, nested ordinal RECD levels (Φ1–Φ3, excess3), λ-weighted RECD, batch orchestration, external Phase-1/Phase-2 FAR scripts, ordinal detector suite under code/ordinal_detectors/.

Companion library: systemictau. Source data: PhysioNet SDDB (DOI 10.13026/C2W306), VFDB, CUDB, NSRDB (raw .dat re-downloadable; annotations and cleaned RR included).

creators.name: Padilla-Villanueva, Johel
creators.affiliation: University of Puerto Rico, Medical Sciences Campus
